response.justification: While the candidate possesses the required technical skills and degree, they are severely overqualified for an entry-level junior position with 15+ years of experience including multiple senior and staff-level roles. Additionally, their German language proficiency (basic) falls short of the 'strong communication' requirement. This represents a significant career regression and poses a high flight risk—the candidate would likely depart quickly once better opportunities emerge. A junior role would neither utilize their expertise nor satisfy someone of their caliber.
strengths: Extensive Java and OOP experience across multiple languages (Java, C++, C#, DLang) with proven enterprise application development
Proven expertise in agile methodologies including TDD, pair programming, CI/CD, and trunk-based development
Strong education background with BS in Computer Science and demonstrated ability to learn new technologies
concerns: German language proficiency is 'basic' when position requires 'strong verbal and written communication in German'
Severely overqualified with 15+ years experience and multiple senior/staff-level positions—high risk of rapid departure
Unexplained career regression from staff engineer to junior role raises questions about motivation and circumstances
